agent_output: {"executive_summary":"A food delivery platform connecting restaurants, customers, and delivery drivers is a complex, multi-sided marketplace with significant architectural implications. However, critical non-functional requirements, scale targets, integration boundaries, and business constraints have not been provided. Without these, recommending a specific architectural pattern (e.g., microservices vs modular monolith, event-driven vs request-response, specific database choices) would be speculative. This analysis maps the known domain structure and identifies the specific constraints needed before architecture decisions can be responsibly made.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Customer Experience","responsibility":"Menu browsing, cart management, order placement, order tracking, payment, ratings/reviews","boundaries":"Customer-facing interactions only; delegates order fulfillment to other components"},{"name":"Restaurant Management","responsibility":"Menu management, order acceptance/rejection, preparation tracking, restaurant onboarding, hours/availability","boundaries":"Restaurant-facing; owns menu and preparation domain"},{"name":"Order Orchestration","responsibility":"Order lifecycle management, state machine for order status, coordination between customer, restaurant, and driver","boundaries":"Core orchestration; does not own payment or delivery logistics directly"},{"name":"Delivery & Logistics","responsibility":"Driver matching/dispatch, route optimization, real-time location tracking, delivery ETAs","boundaries":"Owns driver assignment and geographic/routing concerns"},{"name":"Payment & Billing","responsibility":"Payment processing, refunds, restaurant payouts, driver compensation, promotions/coupons","boundaries":"Financial transactions; likely integrates with external payment providers"},{"name":"Identity & Access","responsibility":"Authentication, authorization, user profiles for all three actor types","boundaries":"Cross-cutting; serves all other components"},{"name":"Notifications","responsibility":"Push notifications, SMS, email for order updates, promotions, driver alerts","boundaries":"Generic supporting service; consumes events from other components"}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Customer Experience","to":"Order Orchestration","contract":"Place order, cancel order, track order status","coupling_type":"synchronous — customer expects immediate confirmation"},{"from":"Order Orchestration","to":"Restaurant Management","contract":"Forward order for acceptance, receive preparation status updates","coupling_type":"could be async (event-driven) or sync depending on latency requirements — UNKNOWN"},{"from":"Order Orchestration","to":"Delivery & Logistics","contract":"Request driver assignment when order is ready or near-ready","coupling_type":"async preferred — driver matching is inherently eventual"},{"from":"Order Orchestration","to":"Payment & Billing","contract":"Authorize payment on order placement, capture on delivery, handle refunds","coupling_type":"synchronous for authorization, async for settlement"},{"from":"Delivery & Logistics","to":"Notifications","contract":"Emit location updates and status changes for real-time tracking","coupling_type":"event-driven / pub-sub"},{"from":"Order Orchestration","to":"Notifications","contract":"Emit order state transitions for customer/restaurant/driver notifications","coupling_type":"event-driven / pub-sub"}],"data_flow":"Customer places order → Order Orchestration authorizes payment → forwards to Restaurant → Restaurant accepts and prepares → Delivery & Logistics assigns driver → Driver picks up and delivers → Payment captured → Ratings collected. Real-time location and status updates flow via events throughout.","patterns_used":[]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Ordering","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: OrderId, contains OrderItems, tracks OrderStatus state machine, references CustomerId, RestaurantId)","Cart (ephemeral, pre-order)"]},{"name":"Restaurant","classification":"core","aggregates":["Restaurant (root: RestaurantId, contains Menu, MenuItems, OperatingHours, AcceptancePolicy)","MenuCategory"]},{"name":"Delivery","classification":"core","aggregates":["Delivery (root: DeliveryId, references OrderId, DriverId, tracks DeliveryStatus, Route)","DriverAvailability (root: DriverId, current location, online/offline status)"]},{"name":"Payment","classification":"generic","aggregates":["PaymentTransaction (root: TransactionId, references OrderId, amount, status)","Payout (root: PayoutId, references RestaurantId or DriverId)"]},{"name":"Identity","classification":"generic","aggregates":["UserAccount (root: UserId, role: customer|restaurant|driver, credentials, profile)"]},{"name":"Notifications","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["NotificationTemplate","NotificationLog"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Ordering","downstream":"Delivery","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Ordering dictates when delivery is needed; Delivery conforms to order lifecycle events"},{"upstream":"Ordering","downstream":"Payment","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Ordering triggers payment authorization and capture"},{"upstream":"Restaurant","downstream":"Ordering","relationship":"Conformist — Ordering references Restaurant's menu model for item validation and pricing"},{"upstream":"Ordering","downstream":"Notifications","relationship":"Open Host Service — Ordering publishes domain events that Notifications consumes"},{"upstream":"Delivery","downstream":"Notifications","relationship":"Open Host Service — Delivery publishes location/status events"}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Cannot recommend microservices vs modular monolith","gains":"N/A","sacrifices":"N/A","alternatives_considered":["Microservices: independent scaling and deployment per context, but operational complexity is high — only justified at significant scale","Modular monolith: simpler operations, faster initial development, can be decomposed later — but couples deployment","Hybrid: start monolith with clean module boundaries, extract hot paths (e.g., delivery tracking) as needed"]},{"decision":"Cannot recommend specific data architecture","gains":"N/A","sacrifices":"N/A","alternatives_considered":["Shared database with schema-per-context: simple, but creates coupling risk","Database-per-service: strong isolation, but distributed transactions become complex (e.g., order + payment atomicity)","Event sourcing for Order aggregate: great audit trail and state reconstruction, but adds complexity"]},{"decision":"Cannot recommend sync vs async communication style","gains":"N/A","sacrifices":"N/A","alternatives_considered":["Request-response for customer-facing paths (low latency)","Event-driven for cross-context coordination (loose coupling)","CQRS for read-heavy paths like menu browsing and order tracking","The right mix depends heavily on latency SLAs and throughput requirements — UNKNOWN"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Order state machine","evolution_path":"New order types (scheduled, group orders, catering) can be added by extending the state machine without changing the core flow"},{"area":"Driver matching algorithm","evolution_path":"Can evolve from simple nearest-driver to ML-based optimization independently of other components"},{"area":"Payment provider","evolution_path":"Anti-corruption layer around payment integration allows swapping providers (Stripe, Adyen, etc.) without domain impact"},{"area":"Notification channels","evolution_path":"New channels (WhatsApp, in-app) added without touching upstream contexts"}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Order lifecycle state machine","reason":"Central to the platform — all three actor types depend on consistent order states. Changes here ripple everywhere."},{"area":"Payment authorization flow","reason":"Financial correctness is non-negotiable. Auth-before-fulfillment pattern must be preserved."},{"area":"Core domain events schema","reason":"Once published, event schemas become contracts. Breaking changes require versioning strategy."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Designing without scale targets may lead to over-engineering (microservices too early) or under-engineering (monolith that can't handle geo-distributed real-time tracking)","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Define expected concurrent users, orders/hour, geographic regions, and latency SLAs before choosing deployment topology"},{"risk":"Real-time driver tracking has fundamentally different infrastructure needs (WebSockets, high-frequency location updates, geospatial queries) than order management (CRUD, state machines)","severity":"high","mitigation":"This is likely the first candidate for independent infrastructure regardless of overall architecture style — but need throughput numbers to size it"},{"risk":"Distributed transactions across Order + Payment + Restaurant acceptance create consistency challenges","severity":"high","mitigation":"Saga pattern is the standard approach, but complexity depends on failure mode requirements — need to know: what happens when restaurant rejects after payment auth? Auto-refund? Hold and retry?"},{"risk":"Multi-sided marketplace creates complex incentive and pricing logic that may not fit cleanly into any single bounded context","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Pricing/promotions may need its own context — depends on business complexity (surge pricing? dynamic delivery fees? restaurant promotions vs platform promotions?)"}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"","files_to_create_or_modify":[],"patterns_to_follow":"Regardless of final architecture: (1) Model Order as a state machine with explicit transitions. (2) Use domain events for cross-context communication. (3) Anti-corruption layer around all external integrations (payment, maps/routing, SMS). (4) Separate read models for customer-facing queries (menu browsing, order tracking) from write models.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do not start with microservices without proven scale requirements — the operational cost is enormous","Do not share a single User model across all three actor types — their concerns diverge significantly","Do not couple real-time tracking infrastructure to order management infrastructure","Do not model delivery fee calculation inside the Order context — it belongs in Delivery or a separate Pricing context","Do not skip idempotency design for payment operations — network failures will cause duplicate charges"]},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_constraints","description":"Cannot make responsible architecture decisions without the following constraints: (1) Scale targets — expected orders/hour, concurrent users, number of restaurants/drivers, geographic regions. (2) Latency SLAs — acceptable response times for order placement, real-time tracking update frequency. (3) Tech stack constraints — team expertise, existing infrastructure, cloud provider preferences. (4) Deployment model — single region vs multi-region, cloud vs hybrid. (5) Business rules — surge pricing? scheduled orders? multi-restaurant orders? tipping model? (6) Integration boundaries — specific payment providers, mapping/routing services, restaurant POS integrations? (7) Reliability requirements — what's the acceptable downtime? What happens during partial outages? (8) Budget and team size — this fundamentally affects whether microservices or monolith is appropriate.","routed_to":"user","options":["Provide NFRs and constraints so I can recommend a specific architecture","Start with a modular monolith assuming moderate scale (<10K orders/day) and refine as constraints emerge","Focus architecture on a specific slice (e.g., just the ordering flow) to reduce scope"],"recommendation":"Provide at minimum: scale targets, latency SLAs, team size, and deployment model. These four alone would unlock a concrete architectural recommendation."}],"confidence":{"level":45,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain model and bounded context boundaries — these hold regardless of technical architecture","Core aggregates and their responsibilities","Key risks around distributed transactions and real-time tracking","Flexibility and rigidity points"],"low_confidence_areas":["Deployment topology (microservices vs monolith vs hybrid)","Communication patterns (sync vs async mix)","Data architecture (shared DB vs DB-per-service vs event sourcing)","Specific technology choices","Infrastructure sizing for real-time tracking"],"assumptions":["This is a new platform (greenfield), not a re-architecture","Standard food delivery model (not ghost kitchens, not grocery, not catering-only)","Three distinct actor types with separate interfaces (customer app, restaurant dashboard, driver app)","Payment is handled via third-party provider, not built in-house"]}}

The user wants me to design an architecture for a food delivery platform. Let me classify this first.

Classification: `missing_constraints` — I have no NFRs, scale requirements, integration boundaries, tech stack preferences, deployment targets, or business constraints. "Food delivery platform connecting restaurants, customers, and delivery drivers" is extremely broad with no constraints provided.

I need to escalate for missing constraints rather than guess at an architecture.
